name: You

anxiety_profile.impact: cannot focus on anything else until meeting is over

work_mode_imbalance.weakness: execution (repetitive loops, ongoing maintenance, sustained execution)
work_mode_imbalance.natural_pattern: Design depletes budget slowly; execution depletes fast

cognitive_context_switching.what_this_means: holding multiple layers of mental models simultaneously
cognitive_context_switching.why: context switch breaks the layered abstraction layers being held in working memory
cognitive_context_switching.true_cost: Each switch loses the abstraction layer; must rebuild from scratch
cognitive_context_switching.phone_interruption_cost: HIGH (completely breaks layer; phone can't be in peripheral awareness)
cognitive_context_switching.meeting_context_switch: CATASTROPHIC (you lose the design layer; need 30min+ to rebuild)

phone_windows.purpose: Check messages after morning deep work

actions: Phone in kitchen or inaccessible location
protect_peak_focus.violations: Lose entire day's focus window; rest of day is lower quality

context_switch_cap.action: STOP accepting new tasks; finish current task
context_switch_cap.signal: Noticing yourself jumping between things; losing focus
context_switch_cap.response: Set timer; complete current task; no new tasks until timer ends

overstimulation_boundary.action: No deep work after 3pm; light work or STOP
overstimulation_boundary.recovery: 1-2 hours low-stress activity (walk, read, async admin)
overstimulation_boundary.violation_cost: Next day's focus degraded; requires 2x recovery

meeting_density_limit.action: Recovery break required after (30-60 min solo time)
meeting_density_limit.blocking: Decline 3+ meetings in a row; suggest async alternative

phone_discipline.rationale: Phone usage spikes as focus breaks down; batching prevents constant checking

anxiety_management.impact: cannot focus on anything until meeting is over
anxiety_management.strategy: minimize total meeting load + mandatory recovery blocks
actions: Max 1 meeting/bilateral per day (2-3 per week)
No back-to-back meetings; minimum 2-hour recovery between
Batch all meetings into single morning slot if possible (e.g., 11am-12pm)
Schedule 2-4 hour recovery block immediately after meeting
During recovery: offline, no decisions, light activities only
Day with meetings: abandon deep work goals; focus on recovery
anxiety_management.violation_cost: Anxiety spiral; cannot focus on anything; day is lost; recovery extends to next day

daily_schedule_recommendation: NO-MEETING DAYS: Option A (Protect Peak). MEETING DAYS: Modified schedule (see below)

task_examples: Get something meaningful done before anxiety hits
rules: Stop work at 10:45am to prep for meeting
Take 15 min break before meeting (breathing, grounding)

11am-12pm.anxiety_level: HIGH (cannot focus on anything else until over)

task_examples: Walk outside (30-60 min, away from phone/email)
Gentle admin or routine tasks ONLY
rules: Phone: turned off or in drawer
If still anxious at 2pm: extend recovery
Goal: return to baseline, not accomplish work
12pm-4pm.why: Anxiety from meeting prevents focus on anything else. Recovery is not optional; it's mandatory.

task_examples: If still anxious: continue recovery (walk, read, offline)

meetings_and_bilaterals.route_to: STRICTLY LIMITED to 1 per day (2-3 per week)
meetings_and_bilaterals.total_cost_per_meeting: 120 min meeting + 120-240 min recovery = 240-360 min total
meetings_and_bilaterals.anxiety_impact: HIGH - cannot focus on anything else until meeting is over
meetings_and_bilaterals.strategy: Minimize total meeting load. Batch when possible (e.g., all meetings in one morning). Mandatory recovery after each.

collaborative_work_async.note: Replace synchronous meetings with async alternatives when possible

interrupt_driven.route_to: BATCH to phone windows OR DECLINE

is_interrupt_driven.yes: Decline if possible; batch to phone windows if necessary (emulated fit)

can_it_wait.yes: Defer to tomorrow or next week

is_it_critical.yes: Do it; then block recovery time

10am-12pm.implementation: Phone in drawer; timer; back to drawer when timer rings
10am-12pm.rationale: Batch checking; prevent constant context switching

12pm-3pm.implementation: Phone in drawer; timer; back to drawer when timer rings
12pm-3pm.rationale: Afternoon decline; minimize interrupts; batch communication

3pm+.rationale: Already overstimulated; phone adds nothing but noise

non_negotiables: 6-10am must be protected: airplane mode, phone away, no notifications
NO MORE THAN 1 MEETING/BILATERAL PER DAY (2-3 per week maximum)
MANDATORY 2-4 hour recovery block IMMEDIATELY after any meeting
During recovery: offline, no work, no decisions, light activities only
No deep work after 3pm: respect your overstimulation boundary
Context switch limit: max 2-3 per hour; stop accepting new tasks when exceeded
Phone windows: scheduled times only; no checking outside windows
Meeting anxiety is non-negotiable constraint: design around it, not through it
Weekly review: Friday reflection on constraint violations; adjust if needed
monthly_review_checklist: Meeting load: Did I stay at 1 per day, 2-3 per week? (If exceeded: impact on anxiety and focus?)
Recovery blocks: After each meeting, did I take 2-4 hour recovery? (If skipped: cost to next days?)
Anxiety: On meeting days, could I focus on anything? (If no: normal, not a failure)
Did I protect 6-10am every day? (If no, why?)
Did I work past 3pm? (How often? Cost?)
Did I hit the phone window schedule? (Slips?)
Energy level: confirm peak at 6-10am, decline by 3pm?
Meeting anxiety: Getting better, worse, or stable? (Trend?)
Violations: What got in the way? Remove obstacles? Renegotiate meeting expectations?
quarterly_rebase_questions: Meeting anxiety: Has it improved, worsened, or stayed the same? (Is the 2-3 per week limit still necessary?)
Recovery requirement: Still need 2-4 hours after meetings? (More/less?)
Can you now focus on other things during meeting anxiety? (Or still completely blocked?)
Is 6-10am still your peak focus window? (Or has it shifted?)
Is 3pm still your overstimulation point? (Or later/earlier?)
Do you have the same attention issues? (Improved by better meeting management?)
Phone usage: still a problem at 3pm+? (Better?)
notes: CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: Meeting anxiety (bilateral/group) prevents focus on anything else until meeting ends. This is the dominant constraint on your workday, not just energy. Strategy: minimize meetings (1 per day max, 2-3 per week). Every meeting requires 2-4 hour recovery block. Design meeting days differently from no-meeting days. Peak focus 6-10am (native fit for deep work). Sequential attention means context switches are expensive. Afternoon decline by 3-4pm. Solution: protect peak window, limit meetings to 1-2 per week, give mandatory recovery after meetings, hard stop by 3pm. Problem is not willpower; it's architecture (sequential OS + high meeting anxiety + low social bandwidth). Redesign workload around this reality.
